description:
# Always cont-fn-wrap multi_clause bodies; let inline_tail_calls_once merge

## The smell

lower_multi_clause has two control-flow models for clause bodies:
- inline-body fast path (when body doesn't cps-split)
- cont-fn wrap (when body might cps-split)

The fast path exists to keep goldens byte-identical with pre-fz-qbg.2
behavior. It's bookkeeping for a constraint that no longer matters:
we now care about meaningfully better CLIF, not byte-identical.

lower_case / lower_with / lower_cond ALREADY always cont-fn-wrap. The
fork is unique to lower_multi_clause.

## What changes

In lower_multi_clause (~line 1096): delete the `if
body_might_cps_split(&clause.body)` branch. Always mint a per-clause
cont fn and TailCall it. Delete body_might_cps_split + its walker.

## What stays

- All semantics. cont-fn wrap is behavior-identical.
- inline_tail_calls_once already merges single-call-site cont fns
  whose body is `Return(param)` back into their caller. Final CLIF
  after that pass should be ≤ today for non-cps-splitting bodies.

## What's removed

- body_might_cps_split fn (~30 LOC walker)
- the fork in lower_multi_clause (~20 LOC)
- One mental model: \"sometimes inline, sometimes cont-fn\"

## Acceptance

- All multi-clause fixtures pass (three-path parity)
- CLIF post-inline-tail-calls-once: ≤ today for every fixture
  (review each diff; meaningful regressions are stop-and-investigate)
- Net LOC reduction in ir_lower.rs
- No new tests needed (existing fixtures cover)

## Risk

inline_tail_calls_once not merging some shape we didn't anticipate →
fixture's CLIF gets bigger. Mitigation: if any fixture regresses,
investigate the unmerged shape, fix the inliner OR document why this
shape resists inlining and accept the cost.

## Why before D.1

D.0 isolates the goldens-churn cause (always-cont-fn) from the matrix
wiring (D.1). Reviewing the two diffs separately is much easier than
reviewing them combined.

comments.text:
WRONG ASSUMPTION — body_might_cps_split fast path is NOT goldens bookkeeping.

inline_tail_calls_once.is_inlinable requires is_leaf — no Call/TailCall in the body. Recursive multi_clause bodies (count, fib_tailrec, eval, etc.) end in TailCall and are NOT inlinable. So always-cont-fn permanently bloats every recursive multi_clause: each clause's tail call is wrapped in an extra fn boundary the inliner cannot remove.

Tried it: ast_eval + 71 fixtures churned, +2557 lines net (CLIF GREW).

CONCLUSION: the fork stays. D.1's matrix dispatch must preserve body_might_cps_split per leaf, just as today's per-clause cascade does.

DELETING this ticket. D.1's design absorbs the per-leaf fork decision in its body callback.

title: fz-ul4.43.D.0: drop body_might_cps_split fast path
